trajectory_summary: The call opened with an engaged, research-primed customer and ended with a booked appointment — but the path between those two points was a seven-minute scope-creep detour that Carter never controlled. Jody drove the agenda from 02:43 onward, pulling Carter into live panel-count arithmetic, local code constraints, and output feasibility questions that belong entirely to the closer. The booking is real but fragile: Jody has unresolved concerns about whether solar is even viable for her roof, and the closer will walk into a customer who has been told specific panel counts by the setter — counts Carter himself admitted he was calculating incorrectly.
outcome_dispute: The v1 tag is unknown. The appointment is booked and should be tagged as such, but it carries conditional risk. Carter completed phone_appointment_specified (the call is explicitly set as a phone call) and text_followup_promise (10:17: 'I'll send you a text confirmation here in a little bit'). However, three button-up protocol elements were absent: one_hour_block_stated, no_driving_at_computer, and is_that_fair_handshake. The booking is structurally incomplete per schema.button_up, and the closer inherits a customer whose feasibility concerns were engaged but not resolved.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Scope-creep trigger recognition and the universal escape hatch
primary_coaching_focus.quote: one of the things you said upfront immediately gives me pause.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Every minute of scope creep on this call traces back to Carter's failure to redirect at 02:43. Jody gave him a clear signal that she had a technical concern — and Carter engaged it rather than bridging. The result was seven minutes of panel arithmetic, an admitted calculation error, and a closer who now inherits a customer with unresolved feasibility doubts and a specific (incorrect) panel count in her head. This is the single highest-leverage coaching moment on the call: if Carter learns to recognize the scope-creep trigger and deploy the escape hatch, the entire call shape changes.

key_moments.quote: one of the things you said upfront immediately gives me pause. You said that, power would be, provided by you guys primarily
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the root-cause moment of the call's scope-creep spiral. Jody flags a specific concern about grid connectivity — she heard Carter's explanation as implying Dominion would be disconnected. Carter's response is to clarify and engage the technical premise rather than acknowledge and bridge. Strategic hypothesis: Carter is building rapport by meeting Jody on her technical level before redirecting. Reactive hypothesis: Carter has no redirect instinct and simply follows the customer's lead. The reactive read holds — no redirect attempt follows for the next four-plus minutes, and Carter ends up doing live panel arithmetic.

Carter is doing live panel-count arithmetic on a call where his stated job is scheduling. He has already admitted at 04:23 that he doesn't know the answer ('I'm not too sure. I just dealt with the scheduling side of things') — and then continues calculating anyway. This is scope creep compounded by inaccuracy: Carter later revises his own count ('It's only nine panels. Yeah. I'm not doing that right') at 07:06. The reactive read is unambiguous — there is no strategic setup here.

key_moments.quote: I'd love to just get your utility bill so we know how many panels you would need, and then just send it over to my design team.
key_moments.stewart_read: Carter's first redirect to the verify/bill-collection path comes at 07:14 — more than seven minutes into the call. This is a late recovery, not a planned move. The redirect itself is clean and lands without resistance, which is worth acknowledging. But the timing means Carter spent the entire opening of the call in scope-creep territory before attempting the move that should have come at minute two.

key_moments.quote: Okay. I mean, we could just build that off square footage as well.
key_moments.stewart_read: When Jody reveals she hasn't moved in yet and won't have a utility bill for two months, Carter pivots immediately to square footage as a design input. This is a genuine positive recovery — Carter finds a workable path around a qualification gap without losing the booking. The move is reactive in origin (he didn't anticipate the pre-move situation) but the execution is clean and keeps the call moving.
